message:
fix(h2): prevent connection window leak on closed-stream DATA and fix GOAWAY stream reset scope

Connection DATA handling:
- Move connection-level WINDOW_UPDATE check to run immediately after
  decrementing recv_window, BEFORE the stream lookup. Previously, DATA
  frames arriving for already-closed/pruned streams would trigger a
  stream error return that bypassed the window replenishment, permanently
  leaking connection-level flow control capacity. Over time this leads
  to connection deadlocks as the peer's send window shrinks to zero.

GOAWAY handling:
- Only reset locally-initiated streams above last_stream_id when
  processing GOAWAY. The peer's last_stream_id only restricts streams
  initiated by the receiver, not server-pushed streams. This prevents
  incorrectly resetting server-push streams that the peer has accepted.

Stream window overflow:
- Return stream-scoped H2Error (with stream ID and FlowControlError
  code) from update_send_window/update_recv_window instead of a generic
  connection-level flow_control error. This allows the connection to
  reset only the offending stream rather than tearing down the entire
  connection on a single stream's window overflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
